title: elymus obtusiflorus
intro: Elymus obtusiflorus, also known as rough wheat grass or rough wild rye, is a type of perennial grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. Elymus obtusiflorus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusement, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
